per_outcome.operationalization: Count of distinct fraud risk factors listed by each participant, regardless of relevance or quality.
Count of risks falling into categories rated as highly relevant by 10 expert auditors with average 23 years experience.
Count of risks falling into categories rated as irrelevant by expert auditors (lower is better quality).
Weighted relevance score averaging all identified risks on 1-4 scale, where 1=irrelevant and 4=highly relevant.
Time in seconds from task start to completion of the fraud risk assessment task.
Percentage of participants who successfully identified the fraud scenario embedded in the case.
Hours budgeted for audit procedures in response to identified fraud risks in the modified audit plan.
Number of days post-fiscal-year-end designated for testing cash receipts and sales returns in the audit plan.
Percentage of sampled transactions drawn from the highest-risk population in the audit plan.
